claim_text: CRISPR-Cas9 was first used to edit human embryos in 2015 by a Chinese research group led by Junjiu Huang at Sun Yat-sen University, who reported the work in the journal Protein & Cell.

gold_sources.citation: Liang, P., Xu, Y., Zhang, X. et al. CRISPR/Cas9-mediated gene editing in human tripronuclear zygotes. Protein Cell 6, 363–372 (2015).
gold_sources.supports: Primary report. Confirms Sun Yat-sen, Junjiu Huang lead author, Protein & Cell, 2015, human (tripronuclear) zygotes.

distractor_sources.note: Many CRISPR papers from 2013-2014 about non-human targets exist; agent should not cite these as the 'first human embryo' claim.

notes: Smoke test case. Claim is well-attested and verifiable from a single primary source. An LLM with web search should hit this near-perfectly. Used to confirm the harness shape works end-to-end before harder cases.
